---
title: "Tutorial: Mastering Lineage Tracking"
description: >
  Learn automatic and manual dependency tracking for complete provenance
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE)
```

## Why Lineage Matters

When a reviewer asks *"which normalized counts did you use for Figure 3?"* or a collaborator asks *"did this result use the batch-corrected or uncorrected data?"*, lineage provides the answer automatically.

OmicsLake tracks **dataset-level dependencies**: which tables were used to produce which outputs.

## Automatic Tracking with dplyr

### How it works

When you call `lake$ref("name")`, OmicsLake returns a **lazy reference** with a `lake_tbl` S3 class. This class carries source metadata through all dplyr operations:

```r
# ref() attaches source metadata
tbl <- lake$ref("counts")    # class: lake_tbl, source: "counts"

# dplyr verbs preserve it
filtered <- tbl |>
  dplyr::filter(gene_type == "protein_coding")
# class: lake_tbl, source: "counts"   (preserved!)

# Joins merge sources from both tables
joined <- tbl |>
  dplyr::left_join(lake$ref("metadata"), by = "sample_id")
# class: lake_tbl, source: c("counts", "metadata")   (merged!)
```

### save_as() records the edges

The `save_as()` pipe terminator persists the result and writes lineage edges:

```r
lake$ref("raw_counts") |>
  dplyr::left_join(lake$ref("sample_info"), by = "sample_id") |>
  dplyr::filter(quality_score > 0.8) |>
  dplyr::group_by(condition) |>
  dplyr::summarize(mean_expr = mean(expression)) |>
  save_as("condition_summary", lake)
```

This records two edges: `raw_counts → condition_summary` and `sample_info → condition_summary`.

### Visualizing the graph

```r
lake$tree("condition_summary")
# condition_summary
#   ├── raw_counts
#   └── sample_info

# Upstream: what does this dataset depend on?
deps("condition_summary", direction = "upstream")

# Downstream: what depends on raw_counts?
deps("raw_counts", direction = "downstream")
```

## Understanding the Lineage Graph

Lineage is stored in the `__ol_dependencies` table with edges like:

```
child_name          parent_name       relationship_type
condition_summary   raw_counts        derived_from
condition_summary   sample_info       derived_from
de_results          condition_summary derived_from
```

The `tree()` method performs breadth-first traversal to reconstruct the full DAG:

```
raw_counts ──┐
              ├──→ condition_summary ──→ de_results ──→ volcano_data
sample_info ──┘
```

## Manual Annotation with depends_on

### When you need it

Automatic tracking only works with dplyr operations on `lake_tbl` objects. You need manual annotation for:

- **Bioconductor functions**: DESeq2, edgeR, limma, Seurat
- **Base R operations**: `merge()`, `[` subsetting, `apply()`
- **After `collect()`**: Once materialized, lineage metadata is lost

### Basic usage

```r
# After running DESeq2
dds <- DESeq2::DESeq(lake$get("count_matrix"))
res <- DESeq2::results(dds)

lake$put("deseq_results", as.data.frame(res),
         depends_on = "count_matrix")
```

### Multiple parents

```r
# Integration that depends on three sources
lake$put("integrated", integrated_data,
         depends_on = c("rnaseq_normalized", "proteomics_filtered", "metadata"))
```

### Best practice: annotate at package boundaries

```r
# ✓ Good: annotate right after Bioconductor function
raw <- lake$get("raw_counts")
normalized <- edgeR::calcNormFactors(edgeR::DGEList(counts = raw))
lake$put("normalized", as.data.frame(normalized$counts),
         depends_on = "raw_counts")

# ✗ Bad: forget to annotate
raw <- lake$get("raw_counts")
normalized <- edgeR::calcNormFactors(edgeR::DGEList(counts = raw))
lake$put("normalized", as.data.frame(normalized$counts))
# Lineage edge is missing!
```

## Lightweight Mode

For existing scripts you don't want to rewrite, three options:

### observe() --- non-invasive session tracking

```r
session <- observe(lake)

raw <- lake$get("counts")
normalized <- normalize(raw)
lake$put("normalized", normalized)

session$finish()
# Operations logged; dependencies inferred from get/put pairs
```

### wrap_fn() --- wrap existing functions

```r
tracked_deseq <- wrap_fn(function(counts) {
  dds <- DESeq2::DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(counts, colData, ~condition)
  DESeq2::DESeq(dds)
}, reads = "counts", writes = "deseq_results")

tracked_deseq(lake$get("counts"))
```

### with_tracking() --- context manager

```r
with_tracking(lake, {
  counts <- lake$get("raw_counts")
  metadata <- lake$get("sample_info")

  merged <- merge(counts, metadata, by = "sample_id")
  filtered <- merged[merged$quality > 0.8, ]

  lake$put("qc_filtered", filtered)
})
# Dependencies: raw_counts → qc_filtered, sample_info → qc_filtered
```

## Common Patterns

### Pattern 1: Mixed dplyr + Bioconductor pipeline

```r
# Step 1: dplyr preprocessing (auto-tracked)
lake$ref("raw_counts") |>
  dplyr::filter(total_count > 10) |>
  save_as("filtered_counts", lake)

# Step 2: DESeq2 (manual annotation)
dds <- DESeq2::DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = lake$get("filtered_counts"),
  colData = lake$get("sample_info"),
  design = ~condition
)
res <- DESeq2::results(DESeq2::DESeq(dds))
lake$put("de_results", as.data.frame(res),
         depends_on = c("filtered_counts", "sample_info"))

# Step 3: dplyr post-processing (auto-tracked)
lake$ref("de_results") |>
  dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1) |>
  save_as("significant_genes", lake)

lake$tree("significant_genes")
# significant_genes
#   └── de_results
#         ├── filtered_counts
#         │     └── raw_counts
#         └── sample_info
```

### Pattern 2: Multi-branch analysis

```r
# Two analysts, same starting data
lake$ref("normalized") |>
  dplyr::filter(gene_biotype == "protein_coding") |>
  save_as("protein_coding", lake)

lake$ref("normalized") |>
  dplyr::filter(gene_biotype == "lncRNA") |>
  save_as("lncrna", lake)

# Both tracked back to the same source
lake$tree("protein_coding")  # → normalized
lake$tree("lncrna")          # → normalized
```

## Troubleshooting Lineage

### Missing edges

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| `save_as()` shows no parents | Used `get()` instead of `ref()` | Use `lake$ref()` for tracked references |
| Edge missing after `collect()` | dplyr ops after materialization | Call `save_as()` before or right after `collect()` |
| Bioconductor step has no parents | No `depends_on` specified | Add `depends_on` parameter to `put()` |

### Verifying completeness

```r
# Check all dependencies of a dataset
deps("final_result", direction = "upstream", max_depth = 10)

# Manually add a missing edge
link("child_dataset", "parent_dataset")

# Remove an incorrect edge
unlink_dep("child_dataset", "wrong_parent")
```

## Next Steps

- **[Reproducibility Tutorial](tutorial-reproducibility.html)** --- Version control strategies
- **[RNA-seq Scenario](scenario-rnaseq.html)** --- Complete workflow with lineage
- **[FAQ](faq.html)** --- Common questions about lineage tracking
